recent_examples.title: The "Story" as Signal:
recent_examples.idea: The concept that the *willingness* to complete a high-effort "story" reflection is itself a stronger signal of candidate quality than the content of the resume.

recent_examples.title: Separation of Concerns:
recent_examples.idea: V's observation that having a "separation between who deals with the candidate and who deals with the employer" creates a better psychological space for candidates to be honest and reflective.

recent_examples.title: Career as a "Diet"
recent_examples.idea: V's analogy of hiring/job seeking as a diet consisting of a mix of standard opportunities and "outsized bets" (the 40-yard screamer).

recent_examples.title: Model Asymmetry:
recent_examples.idea: The idea that using "the absolute dumbest models" (4o-mini) in multiple small steps can produce a more "intelligent" and certainly more resilient output than single-shot high-end models.

recent_examples.title: Low-Fidelity Architecture Strength:
recent_examples.idea: Building sophisticated outputs from "dumb" mini-models to ensure architecture resilience—counter-intuitive in an era where everyone is rushing toward the most expensive, frontier models.
